title: Clytostoma ocositense
taxonomy: Clytostoma ocositense is classified as a species within the genus Clytostoma of flowering plants in the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape or Virginia creeper family. This species is native to tropical regions, specifically in Central and South America. Clytostoma ocositense is a member of the tribe Cissinae, which is further divided into several genera, including Clytostoma. intro: Clytostoma ocositense, also known as the Panama hat plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The health benefits of Clytostoma ocositense include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
